title: Weather forecast on Monday 24 February
description: GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - In the latest...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE | KAROO NEWS - In the latest regional weather forecast from the South South African Weather Services, a cool day can be expected along the Garden Route with warmer weather over the Karoo.


> Impact based warnings


Yellow Level 2 Warning: Severe thunderstorms with heavy downpours and strong to damaging winds leading to localised flooding of roads and settlements as well as localised damage to infrastructure are expected over the northern and western parts of Limpopo.

Yellow level 2 warning: Disruptive rain leading to localised flooding of susceptible formal and informal settlements, roads and bridges and localised damage to infrastructure is expected along the extreme north-eastern parts of KwaZulu Natal.

Yellow level 1 warning: Damaging wind leading to difficulty in navigation at sea, is expected between Lambert's Bay and Cape Point from Monday afternoon, spreading to Cape Agulhas from Tuesday afternoon and clearing north of Table Bay from Wednesday.


> Fire danger warnings


Extremely high fire danger conditions are expected over Hantam, Khai-Ma and Nama Khoi municipalities of the Northen Cape as well as Cederberg municipality of the Western Cape.


> Your weather on Monday


Western Cape: Fine over the western parts, otherwise cloudy and warm to hot but cool along the south-west and south coast with light rain along the south coast. The wind along the coast will be fresh to strong south-easterly along the west coast, but light to moderate easterly along the south coast becoming moderate to fresh southerly from the afternoon.

Western half of the Eastern Cape: Fine in the extreme north, otherwise partly cloudy and cool to warm with isolated rain and showers in the south where it will be cloudy. Isolated thundershowers can be expected in the north-west by the afternoon. The wind along the coast will be light to moderate south-easterly becoming southerly in the evening.

Eastern half of the Eastern Cape: Fine in the northwest at first, otherwise partly cloudy and warm, but cloudy and cool with isolated showers and rain in places along and south of the escarpment. The wind along the coast will be light to moderate south-westerly north of East

London at first, otherwise south-easterly, becoming easterly in the evening.

Gauteng: Partly cloudy and warm with scattered showers and thundershowers.

The UVB index: Very High

Mpumalanga: Morning fog patches along the escarpment, otherwise cloudy and cool to warm with isolated showers and thundershowers but scattered in the west where it will be partly cloudy. It will be hot in the Lowveld.

Limpopo: Morning fog patches along the escarpment, otherwise cloudy and warm

with scattered showers and thundershowers but isolated in the south-east.

North West: Partly cloudy and cool to warm, with isolated showers and thundershowers over the central parts, but scattered in the east.

Free State: Partly cloudy and cool to warm, with isolated showers and thundershowers over the central parts, but scattered in the east.

Northern Cape: Fine and warm to hot becoming partly cloudy over the east and the interior. The wind along the coast will be light to moderate southerly to south-easterly becoming fresh from the afternoon.

KwaZulu-Natal: Morning fog over the interior, otherwise cloudy and warm but cool in the south-west. Isolated showers and thundershowers are expected but scattered over the extreme north-east. The wind along coast will be moderate southerly to south-easterly.


> Long term forecast for Tuesday and Wednesday


‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
